workflow_name: Execute a SQL SELECT Statement via DB2 Admin Edit/Run SQL

steps.description: Type option 2 to select Execute SQL Statements on the DB2 Admin main menu
Press Enter to navigate to the Execute SQL Statements menu
Type option 1 to select Edit/run SQL statements
Press Enter to open the Edit/Run SQL Statement editor
Type the SELECT clause of the SQL statement on the first blank input line in the editor
steps.placeholder: [SELECT Clause, e.g. SELECT * or SELECT column1, column2]

steps.description: Type the FROM clause with the schema-qualified table name on the second blank input line
Press Enter to submit the SQL text and render line numbers in the editor
Type EXEC in the Command field to execute the SQL statement
Press Enter to run the SQL statement and display the Browse Result
Press PF11 to scroll right and view additional columns in the result set
Press PF10 to scroll left and return to the first set of columns
Press PF3 to exit the result browser and return to the SQL editor
Press PF3 again to exit the SQL editor; a confirmation dialog will appear asking whether to run the statement
Type 1 in the confirmation dialog to select No (do not run the SQL statement) and exit the editor
Press Enter to confirm the choice and return to the Execute SQL Statements menu
Press PF3 to return to the DB2 Admin main menu
